title: Undo, Restore, Revert, Reset, and Reflog Recovery · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Recover from an incorrect edit or commit using the least destructive operation and a verified recovery reference. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.
Use restore for working-tree and index paths with explicit sources, patch selection, and pre-change diffs.
Create and verify inverse commits for published changes, merges, and later dependencies without claiming history was erased.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Undo Layer Choice · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

Which action demonstrates Undo Layer Choice without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.

content.text: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

content.title: Evidence inspection
content.text: Use the supplied trace to distinguish an observed Undo Layer Choice result from an unsupported claim.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Pro Git, Second Edition — Controls cumulative conceptual sequence from setup through internals, collaboration, tooling, and distributed workflows.

hints: Name the Undo Layer Choice precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.

stimulus.caption: Inspect the observation, governing requirement, and mastery evidence before deciding.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence.

options.text: Select an undo operation from affected layer, publication state, collaboration, desired topology, and recovery evidence. Apply it to the stated input and retain a reproducible trace for an undo decision matrix and disaster-recovery lab.
There is one Git undo command that safely applies to every mistake.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Undo Layer Choice evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Undo Layer Choice assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

quiz case — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Reflog Object Recovery: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — One unstaged edit, one staged file, local commit a11ce00, and published commit b00b1e5 each need different recovery. Preserve a temporary ref and choose restore, reset, revert, or reflog per layer.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use reflogs, dangling-object inspection, temporary references, and expiry limits to recover otherwise unreachable work.
